paper_title: Turán graphs with bounded matching number
summary: Gerbner (arXiv 2211.03272, J. Graph Theory 2024) directly took up this problem immediately after Alon–Frankl's paper, determining the maximum number of edges in n-vertex F-free graphs with matching number at most s apart from a constant additive term for all fixed F and s, with several exact results also obtained. The asymptotic form of the answer is thus settled for fixed parameters, but a complete exact determination for all H, n, s remains open. Ma and Hou (arXiv 2301.05625) subsequently extended the framework to the generalized Turán setting (counting copies of K_r).

since_posted.title: On Turán problems with bounded matching number
since_posted.claim: Determines the maximum number of edges in n-vertex F-free graphs with matching number at most s apart from a constant additive term for all fixed F and s, providing several exact results; directly extends the Alon–Frankl program to general forbidden subgraphs H.

since_posted.title: Generalized Turán problem with bounded matching number
since_posted.claim: Extends the bounded-matching-number framework to the generalized Turán problem, determining ex(n, K_r, {K_{k+1}, M_{s+1}}) exactly for r ≥ 3 and providing O(1)-error bounds for general H.

search_queries: H-free graph matching number Turán edges maximum Alon Frankl 2022 arXiv 2210.15076
H-free graphs bounded matching number maximum edges color-critical forbidden subgraph
notes: Gerbner's paper 2211.03272 was submitted just 12 days after the Alon–Frankl paper and is the principal follow-up; it is published in J. Graph Theory (DOI 10.1002/jgt.23067). The asymptotic answer for fixed F and s is settled, so the status is 'partial' rather than 'open'. Full exact determination for all parameters and all H remains open.
